name: Nobody Talks About This
trigger: Implies hidden info everyone else is missing
template: Nobody talks about [SURPRISING THING] about [TOPIC]
why_it_works: Frames the content as exclusive insider knowledge others overlooked.
examples: Nobody talks about this part of the journey.
Why does nobody talk about this?
Nobody talks about what actually happens after you quit.
The secret nobody talks about...
Nobody talks about this until it finally works.

applied_examples.hook: Nobody talks about what really happens to your body in week 3.
Nobody talks about the one review that kills small businesses.

name: I Tried So You Don't Have To
trigger: Offers a shortcut to others' costly experimentation
template: I tried [THING/NUMBER OF THINGS] so you don't have to
why_it_works: Promises a digested verdict, saving the viewer effort and risk.
examples: I tried this so you don't have to.
I tried every productivity app so you don't have to.
I tried the latest viral trend so you don't have to.
I tested 5 different ones, here's what actually worked.
I tried it for 30 days so you don't have to.

applied_examples.hook: I tried every viral foundation so you don't have to.
I tested 6 air fryers so you don't have to.

name: Wait For It
trigger: Open loop forcing viewers to stay for the payoff
template: Wait for it... / Wait until you see how this ends
why_it_works: Creates an explicit unresolved promise that punishes scrolling away.
examples: Wait for it...
Wait until you see how this ends.
You'll never guess how this one ends.
Wait till the final shot.
Trust me, wait for the end.

applied_examples.hook: If you love pasta, wait for the final shot.
Wait until you see the after.

name: What If I Told You
trigger: Reframes a belief, sparking 'really?' curiosity
template: What if I told you [COUNTERINTUITIVE CLAIM]?
why_it_works: Sets up a surprising reveal that contradicts what viewers assume.
examples: What if I told you it isn't even the problem?
What if I told you the most productive hour is the one you do nothing?
What if I told you you've been doing this wrong for years?
What if I told you the cheap version works better?

applied_examples.hook: What if I told you your savings account is losing you money?
What if I told you cardio is why you're not losing fat?

name: The Real Reason
trigger: Promises the true hidden cause behind a problem
template: The reason your [THING] isn't working? It's not [ASSUMED CAUSE]
why_it_works: Dismisses the obvious explanation, teasing a non-obvious answer.
examples: The reason your content isn't working? It's not the algorithm.
This is why your [thing] isn't working.
The real reason you're stuck has nothing to do with effort.
Here's why you're not growing, and it's not what you think.

applied_examples.hook: The reason your skincare isn't working? It's not your products.
The reason you're not getting clients? It's not your prices.

name: You Won't Believe
trigger: Promises something shocking enough to defy belief
template: You won't believe what happens when [SETUP]
why_it_works: Dares the viewer to verify the unbelievable claim themselves.
examples: You won't believe what happened next.
You won't believe how easy this is once you know how.
You won't believe what happened while filming this.
I can't believe what I just discovered!

applied_examples.hook: You won't believe what came off this carpet.
You won't believe how cheap this hotel was.

name: Everything You Know Is Wrong
trigger: Challenges core assumptions, triggering defensive curiosity
template: Everything you know about [TOPIC] is wrong
why_it_works: Threatens the viewer's existing beliefs so they must hear the correction.
examples: Everything you know about morning routines is wrong.
Everything you knew about [topic] is 100% WRONG!
Everyone is wrong about this.
You've been lied to about [topic].

applied_examples.hook: Everything you know about ab workouts is wrong.
Everything you were taught about saving is wrong.

name: Unpopular Opinion
trigger: Signals a bold dissenting take worth debating
template: Unpopular opinion: [BOLD STATEMENT]
why_it_works: Promises a divisive view that invites both agreement and argument.
examples: Unpopular opinion: most productivity advice is for people who already have time.
This may be controversial but...
Hot take: [statement].
Prove me wrong.

applied_examples.hook: Unpopular opinion: you don't need to post every day.
Unpopular opinion: expensive skincare is a scam.

name: Stop Doing X
trigger: Commands a behavior change, implying you're doing it wrong
template: Stop doing [COMMON PRACTICE], here's what actually works
why_it_works: Direct correction implies the viewer is making an error right now.
examples: Stop doing this one thing, it's holding you back.
Stop tracking macros if you want to lose fat.
Stop making this mistake with your captions.
You need to stop doing this!

applied_examples.hook: Stop doing crunches, do this instead.
Stop boosting posts, here's what actually brings customers.

name: They're Lying To You
trigger: Pits viewer against a deceptive authority figure
template: Other [EXPERTS/BRANDS] are lying to you about [TOPIC]
why_it_works: Casts the creator as the honest insider exposing industry deceit.
examples: Other dentists are lying to you.
You've been lied to.
Don't believe this myth!
Here's what they don't want you to know.

applied_examples.hook: Skincare brands are lying to you about SPF.
Your bank is lying to you about this fee.

name: Don't Believe This Myth
trigger: Names a false belief, promising to debunk it
template: Don't believe the [TOPIC] myth that [FALSE CLAIM]
why_it_works: Labels a common belief as a myth, creating urgency to be corrected.
examples: Don't believe this fitness myth!
The biggest myth about [topic], busted.
This advice you keep hearing is completely false.
The truth about [topic] nobody admits.

applied_examples.hook: Don't believe the myth that carbs make you fat.
Don't believe the myth that multitasking works.

name: You're Doing It Wrong
trigger: Accuses viewer of an error they want to fix
template: You're doing [TASK] wrong, here's why
why_it_works: Personal accusation creates instant self-conscious attention.
examples: You're doing it wrong, let me show you why.
You're wearing it wrong (here's the fix).
You've been cutting onions wrong your whole life.
You will look at this differently by the end.

applied_examples.hook: You're applying concealer wrong, here's the fix.
You're storing your herbs wrong and they're dying.

name: This Mistake Is Costing You
trigger: Loss aversion: an ongoing hidden cost
template: This one mistake is costing you [VALUABLE THING]
why_it_works: Frames inaction as an active, ongoing loss the viewer must stop.
examples: This one simple mistake could be costing you thousands.
This mistake is costing you clients.
This one mistake is costing you sleep.
This is quietly killing your results.

applied_examples.hook: This pricing mistake is costing you half your customers.
This one mistake is costing you all your gains.

name: Don't Make This Mistake
trigger: Warns of an avoidable error before it happens
template: Don't make this mistake when [DOING TASK]
why_it_works: Preemptive warning makes viewers want to dodge the pitfall named.
examples: Don't make this mistake when doing [task].
If you're doing this you're making a huge mistake!
Avoid doing this if you want [result].
If you want [result], avoid doing this!

applied_examples.hook: Don't make this mistake when booking flights.
Don't make this mistake when filling your brows.

name: Red Flags To Watch For
trigger: Threat detection: spotting danger signs
template: [NUMBER] red flags to look for in [SITUATION]
why_it_works: Taps the instinct to identify and avoid threats before harm.
examples: Red flags to look for in [situation].
3 signs that you should walk away.
Warning signs nobody tells you about.
Here are 3 signs that you should [action].

applied_examples.hook: 5 red flags to look for before you sign a lease.
Red flags to spot in a job interview.

name: This Strategy Helped X People
trigger: Numbers validate that the method actually works
template: This [STRATEGY] helped [NUMBER] of my [AUDIENCE] do [RESULT]
why_it_works: Concrete counts make the claim feel proven rather than promised.
examples: This single email script booked 47 sales calls last month.
This helped 10,000 people do [result].
This strategy got my clients 2x their leads.
Why 10,000 freelancers are switching to this.

applied_examples.hook: This plan helped 300 of my clients drop their first 10 pounds.
This script booked 47 appointments for my salon clients.

name: This Broke The Internet
trigger: Bandwagon: everyone is already reacting to this
template: This [THING] broke the internet / went viral
why_it_works: Implies massive crowd validation you'd be left out of ignoring.
examples: This broke the internet.
This recipe took 15 minutes and went viral.
This blew up way more than I expected.
This edit goes crazy.

applied_examples.hook: This $6 lipstick broke the internet.
This 3-ingredient recipe broke the internet.

name: Everyone Is Doing This
trigger: Bandwagon plus fear of being the only one left out
template: Everyone is doing [THING] except you
why_it_works: Combines social proof with the sting of being behind the crowd.
examples: Everyone is doing this except you.
Every six-figure freelancer is doing this.
Everyone's trying this, but does it actually work?
You're missing out.

applied_examples.hook: Every brand growing right now is doing this one thing.
Everyone with abs is doing this, and it's not crunches.

name: I Almost Gave Up
trigger: Drops viewer into emotional crisis mid-story
template: I almost [BAD OUTCOME] until [TURNING POINT]
why_it_works: Opens an emotional loop at the lowest point, demanding resolution.
examples: I almost gave up, here's what happened next.
I almost quit freelancing six months in, until I changed one thing.
I almost gave up until this.
I thought I failed, but it turned out to be the best thing.

applied_examples.hook: I almost quit the gym for good, until week six.
I almost closed my shop last winter, then this happened.

name: This Is The Story Of
trigger: Narrative framing promises a complete arc
template: This is the story of [PERSON/EVENT]
why_it_works: Story framing triggers our instinct to find out how it ends.
examples: This is the story of how I lost everything.
This is the story of [event].
The day everything changed...
This is what happened when...

applied_examples.hook: This is the story of how I got stranded in Tokyo.
This is the story of how I paid off $40k in a year.

name: The Moment I Realized
trigger: Teases a pivotal realization mid-narrative
template: The moment I realized [INSIGHT]
why_it_works: Hints at a turning-point epiphany the viewer wants to share in.
examples: The moment I realized I was the problem.
The moment I knew it was over.
The day everything changed.
The moment I realized I'd been doing it all wrong.

applied_examples.hook: The moment I realized I was overtraining.
The moment I realized my job was killing me.

name: Numbered Things I Wish I Knew
trigger: Curiosity plus a defined, finite payoff
template: [NUMBER] things I wish I knew before [MILESTONE]
why_it_works: Promises specific, countable lessons with regret-tinged value.
examples: 3 things I wish I knew before doing this.
5 things I wish I knew when I started.
Seven things you wish you knew before launching a course.
5 things nobody tells you about [topic].

applied_examples.hook: 5 things I wish I knew before buying my first home.
3 things I wish I knew before getting lash extensions.

name: Number One Changed My Life
trigger: Teases that a specific list item is the big one
template: [NUMBER] [THINGS], number [X] literally changed my life
why_it_works: Promises a standout item, making viewers watch the whole list.
examples: Five habits that doubled my income, number four was the hardest.
Number one literally changed my life.
3 things that changed my life, the last one shocked me.
5 tips that completely changed everything.

applied_examples.hook: 5 apps I use daily, number 3 saved my business.
4 stretches for back pain, number 2 fixed mine overnight.

name: Numbered Mistakes You're Making
trigger: Combines list payoff with personal error callout
template: [NUMBER] mistakes [AUDIENCE] make with [TOPIC]
why_it_works: Finite list plus the worry that you're guilty of one of them.
examples: 5 mistakes you're probably making when you [task].
3 mistakes new freelancers make in their first 90 days.
3 mistakes you're making right now.
Everyone makes this mistake with [topic].

applied_examples.hook: 4 mistakes killing your Google reviews.
3 mistakes ruining your manicure.

name: Only One Percent Know This
trigger: Exclusivity: rare knowledge few possess
template: Only [SMALL %] of people know this about [TOPIC]
why_it_works: Scarcity of the knowledge makes it feel valuable and elite.
examples: Only 1% of people know this.
This is the only thing you need to know about [topic].
99% of people get this wrong.
Most people will never figure this out.

applied_examples.hook: Only 1% of people use this tax trick.
Only 1% of iPhone users know this setting exists.

name: Did You Know
trigger: Knowledge gap framed as a surprising fact
template: Did you know that [SURPRISING FACT]?
why_it_works: A direct question about a fact you likely don't know demands an answer.
examples: Did you know that [surprising fact]?
I bet you didn't know this about [topic].
Have you ever wondered why...?
Is it just me, or [observation]?

applied_examples.hook: Did you know you've been storing eggs wrong?
Did you know your phone is tracking this?

name: What Would You Do If
trigger: Hypothetical forces viewer to self-insert
template: What would you do if [SCENARIO]?
why_it_works: Engages viewers by making them mentally answer for themselves.
examples: What would you do if you lost your top client tomorrow?
What would you do if [scenario]?
Can you guess what happens next?
If you could only keep one, which would it be?

applied_examples.hook: What would you do if you lost your income tomorrow?
What would you do if your kid said this to you?

name: Are You Tired Of
trigger: Names a pain point the viewer immediately recognizes
template: Are you tired of [PAIN POINT]? Try this
why_it_works: Calls out a frustration so accurately the viewer feels understood.
examples: Are you tired of [issue]? Try this!
Tired of [problem]? Here's the fix.
Struggling with [problem]? Try this.
Do you have problems with [thing]? I found the solution.

applied_examples.hook: Tired of lower back pain? Try this one stretch.
Tired of foundation creasing? Here's the fix.

name: Bold Promise In Timeframe
trigger: Concrete result tied to a specific deadline
template: How I [BIG RESULT] in [SHORT TIMEFRAME]
why_it_works: A specific outcome and timeline makes the promise feel achievable.
examples: How I achieved this in only 3 months.
I grew from 0 to 100K followers in 30 days.
How I fixed this in 3 days.
Here's exactly how to do it in under a week.

applied_examples.hook: How I lost 15 pounds in 8 weeks without the gym.
How I booked out my calendar in 30 days.

name: Best I've Ever Tried
trigger: Superlative implies a must-see standout
template: This is the best [THING] I've ever [USED/TRIED]
why_it_works: An absolute superlative promises peak value worth checking.
examples: The BEST product I've ever used!
This is the best thing I've ever tried.
I promise you've never seen anything like this.
This will blow your mind!

applied_examples.hook: The best drugstore mascara I've ever used.
The best pasta sauce I've ever made, and it's 4 ingredients.

name: This Changed Everything
trigger: Vague but high-stakes transformation tease
template: This one [THING] changed everything about my [AREA]
why_it_works: Promises a dramatic shift while withholding what caused it.
examples: This changed everything for me.
This one mindset shift changes everything.
This hack changed my life!
This one thing saved me.

applied_examples.hook: This one habit changed how I run my whole day.
This one tweak changed my whole training.

name: POV
trigger: Drops viewer into a relatable point-of-view scene
template: POV: [RELATABLE SCENARIO]
why_it_works: Immersive framing makes viewers see themselves in the moment.
examples: POV: you just discovered the productivity app that does it all.
POV: it's Sunday night and you haven't started.
POV: your boss just said 'quick call?'
POV: you finally found the one.

applied_examples.hook: POV: it's leg day and you regret everything.
POV: a customer walks in 2 minutes before closing.

name: Tell Me Without Telling Me
trigger: In-group recognition through shared experience
template: Tell me you [TRAIT] without telling me you [TRAIT]
why_it_works: Rewards viewers who recognize their own in-group instantly.
examples: Tell me you work in marketing without telling me you work in marketing.
Tell me you're a teacher without telling me.
If you've ever worked in retail, you know this pain.
If your [thing] looks like this, same.

applied_examples.hook: Tell me you're a runner without telling me you're a runner.
Tell me you work in restaurants without telling me.

name: You Know That Feeling When
trigger: Names a universal feeling for instant 'that's me'
template: You know that feeling when [RELATABLE MOMENT]?
why_it_works: Articulates a shared experience so viewers feel seen and stay.
examples: You know that feeling when your 'quick task' turns into 3 hours? Yeah, that one.
If you've ever felt invisible at work...
You're not alone if you do this too.
Is it just me, or does everyone do this?

applied_examples.hook: You know that feeling when they finally nap? Yeah.
You know that feeling when payday's gone in a day?

name: As A Professional
trigger: Credentials grant instant trust in the claim
template: As a [PROFESSION] with [X] years, here's my take on [TOPIC]
why_it_works: Stated expertise makes the advice feel credible and worth heeding.
examples: As a UX designer with 8 years experience, here's why most pages don't convert.
As a nurse, here's what I'd never do.
Speaking as someone who's done this 1,000 times...
I've spent 10 years on this, so trust me.

applied_examples.hook: As an esthetician, here's the product I'd never buy.
As an accountant, here's the deduction everyone misses.

name: Let Me Show You How I Actually
trigger: Behind-the-scenes access to a real process
template: Let me show you how I actually [DO PROCESS]
why_it_works: Promises authentic, unfiltered methods instead of generic advice.
examples: Let me show you the spreadsheet I use to plan a month of content.
Here's my go-to setup for this.
Let me show you how I actually do it.
Here's what my routine actually looks like.

applied_examples.hook: Let me show you how I actually price my jobs.
Let me show you how I actually plan my weekly meals.

name: Only X Hours Left
trigger: Deadline scarcity drives immediate action
template: Only [TIME] left to [ACTION/OFFER]
why_it_works: A closing window pressures viewers to act before it's gone.
examples: Only 24 hours left to join the Creator Launchpad.
You don't want to miss this!
Last chance before this is gone.
This ends tonight.

applied_examples.hook: Only 2 spots left for this month.
Only 24 hours left at this price.

name: This Is Your Sign
trigger: Frames the video as a personal cue to act now
template: This is your sign to [ACTION]
why_it_works: Treats the moment as fate, nudging hesitant viewers to commit.
examples: This is your sign to finally start.
This is your sign to book the trip.
Consider this your sign to quit.
If you needed a sign, this is it.

applied_examples.hook: This is your sign to finally sign up.
This is your sign to book the solo trip.

name: Save This For Later
trigger: Implies value so high you'll regret not keeping it
template: Save this, you'll need it when [FUTURE MOMENT]
why_it_works: Signals the content is reference-worthy, prompting saves and watches.
examples: Save this, you'll need it later.
You'll wish you saved this.
Screenshot this before it's gone.
Don't sleep on this.

applied_examples.hook: Save this for the next time you burn the rice.
Save this before tax season hits.

name: From Zero To Result
trigger: Dramatic distance from start to outcome
template: From [LOW STARTING POINT] to [IMPRESSIVE RESULT]
why_it_works: The gap between before and after promises a satisfying journey.
examples: From broke to six figures.
From zero to 100k.
From cluttered desk to dream setup in 20 minutes.
I grew from 0 to 100K followers in 30 days.

applied_examples.hook: From couch to 5k in 8 weeks.
From 0 to fully booked in 60 days.

name: I Tried This For X Days
trigger: Experiment framing promises a documented result
template: I tried [THING] for [X DAYS] and here's what happened
why_it_works: The experiment structure builds anticipation for a real outcome.
examples: I tried this for 30 days, here's what happened.
What I learned after trying it for 30 days.
I did cold showers for a week, this is what changed.
I tried it every day for a month.

applied_examples.hook: I ran every day for 30 days, here's my body now.
I woke up at 5am for 30 days, here's the truth.

name: Before And After Reveal
trigger: Visual contrast creates instant payoff curiosity
template: Watch this [SUBJECT] go from [BEFORE] to [AFTER]
why_it_works: A visible transformation promises satisfying resolution if you stay.
examples: This took 20 minutes and changed everything.
The glow-up you didn't expect.
Watch this transform.
This is what progress really looks like.

applied_examples.hook: Watch this $200 kitchen makeover.
Watch this 5-minute makeover transformation.

name: Stop Scrolling If
trigger: Filters and flags the exact target viewer
template: Stop scrolling if you [WANT/STRUGGLE WITH X]
why_it_works: Directly halts the scroll by naming who the message is for.
examples: Stop scrolling if you want to grow your account.
Stop scrolling, you need to see this.
Stop scrolling if you struggle with sleep.
Don't scroll!

applied_examples.hook: Stop scrolling if you've got knee pain.
Stop scrolling if you own a small business.

name: This Is For You If
trigger: Self-selection: viewer recognizes themselves
template: If you're [TRAIT/STRUGGLE], this is for you
why_it_works: Calling out a specific person makes them feel personally addressed.
examples: If you're struggling with [problem], this is for you.
If you've ever said 'I'll do it tomorrow,' this is for you.
This one's for the overthinkers.
If you're still struggling with this, watch this.

applied_examples.hook: If you're living paycheck to paycheck, this is for you.
If you're a tired mom, this one's for you.

name: Type Of People Stop
trigger: Names a specific tribe to halt their scroll
template: [SPECIFIC GROUP] people, stop scrolling
why_it_works: Direct group address triggers belonging and self-recognition.
examples: Introvert type of people, stop scrolling.
If you're a small business owner, listen up.
This is for the night owls.
Attention all [group].

applied_examples.hook: Skinny guys trying to bulk, stop scrolling.
Oily skin girlies, this is for you.

name: Sudden Motion Interrupt
trigger: Unexpected movement hijacks visual attention
template: Open with abrupt [MOTION/GESTURE] toward camera
why_it_works: Sudden visual change reflexively stops the thumb mid-scroll.
examples: [Jumps into frame] Okay you NEED to see this.
[Snaps fingers] Hey, real quick.
[Lunges at camera] Wait, stop.
[Claps] Listen up.

applied_examples.hook: [Drops into a plank] Bet you can't hold this.
[Slams pan down] You're cooking eggs wrong.

name: Object Destruction Open
trigger: Shocking physical act demands an explanation
template: Open by [BREAKING/RIPPING/POURING] something unexpected
why_it_works: A startling action creates a why-did-they-do-that curiosity gap.
examples: [Rips up resume] Stop sending these.
[Pours out the protein] This is why you're bloated.
[Throws phone on couch] Do this first thing every morning.
[Smashes the jar] You're wasting half of this.

applied_examples.hook: [Cuts up credit card] Do this before you do anything else.
[Tosses the product in trash] Stop using this.

name: Wait, Stop, Don't
trigger: Verbal alarm halts the scroll reflexively
template: Wait. Stop. Don't [ACTION] before you see this
why_it_works: Urgent commands break the passive scroll trance instantly.
examples: Wait, stop, don't buy this until you watch this.
Stop what you're doing.
Hold on, you need to hear this.
No no no, don't do that.

applied_examples.hook: Stop, don't check out until you use this code.
Wait, don't book that hotel yet.

name: Here's How To Exactly
trigger: Promises a precise, actionable method
template: Here's exactly how to [ACHIEVE RESULT]
why_it_works: The word 'exactly' promises a clear, repeatable step-by-step.
examples: Here's exactly how to write a cold email that gets a reply.
Follow this step-by-step process to [result].
Here's a simple hack to help you do [task].
Here's the easiest way to [goal].

applied_examples.hook: Here's exactly how to ask for a 5-star review.
Here's exactly how to fix your squat depth.

name: Simple Hack To
trigger: Promises an easy shortcut to a hard result
template: This simple hack will [SOLVE PROBLEM] in [TIME]
why_it_works: Frames a quick, low-effort trick that feels too easy to skip.
examples: This hack will save you hours on [task].
Here's a simple hack to help you [task].
Try this one hack to get [result].
This 5-minute trick changed how I [task].

applied_examples.hook: This hack peels garlic in 5 seconds.
This simple hack clears your inbox in 10 minutes.

name: I Discovered The Secret
trigger: Promises hidden knowledge being unveiled
template: I discovered the secret to [DESIRED OUTCOME]
why_it_works: The word 'secret' implies privileged info worth staying for.
examples: I discovered the secret to waking up energized.
The secret nobody told me when I started.
I'm about to expose the secret.
Here's the tea.

applied_examples.hook: I discovered the secret to glass skin.
I discovered the secret to never chasing invoices again.

name: What Experts Won't Tell You
trigger: Forbidden knowledge gatekept by insiders
template: Here's what [EXPERTS] won't tell you about [TOPIC]
why_it_works: Implies suppressed truth, making it feel exclusive and rebellious.
examples: Here's what nutritionists won't tell you about shakes.
Here's what they don't want you to know.
Truth bomb incoming.
I'm about to expose [topic].

applied_examples.hook: Here's what your financial advisor won't tell you.
Here's what agents won't tell you about open houses.

name: Instead Of This, Do This
trigger: Side-by-side swap promises a smarter choice
template: Instead of [COMMON CHOICE], do [BETTER ALTERNATIVE]
why_it_works: A direct swap promises an easy upgrade over what you do now.
examples: Instead of buying this, buy this!
If you're doing this, do this instead.
Don't waste energy on this, focus on that.
Swap this for this and watch what happens.

applied_examples.hook: Instead of crunches, do this for abs.
Instead of takeout, make this in 10 minutes.

name: I'm Going To Admit Something
trigger: Vulnerability promises raw, private disclosure
template: I'm going to admit something I've never told anyone
why_it_works: Promised vulnerability feels rare and intimate, holding attention.
examples: I'm going to admit something I've never told anyone, I almost shut down my agency.
I was terrified to share this, but here we go.
Real talk, let's be honest.
This is hard to admit, but...

applied_examples.hook: I'm going to admit something about my 'transformation' photos.
I've never admitted this, but my first year I lost money.

name: My Biggest Mistake Was
trigger: Owning a failure promises an honest lesson
template: My biggest mistake was [MISTAKE], here's what I learned
why_it_works: A confessed failure feels honest and teaches through someone's pain.
examples: My biggest mistake was waiting too long to start.
Here's what I learned the hard way.
I used to hate this until I realized...
I wish someone had told me this sooner.

applied_examples.hook: My biggest money mistake was at 25, learn from it.
My biggest hiring mistake nearly closed my shop.

name: Imagine If
trigger: Aspiration: vivid dream scenario pulls you in
template: Imagine [WAKING UP TO / HAVING] [DREAM SCENARIO]
why_it_works: Painting the ideal outcome makes viewers crave the how-to.
examples: Imagine waking up and seeing 12 new clients booked overnight.
Imagine never having to do this again.
Picture this: [dream scenario].
What if your mornings looked like this?

applied_examples.hook: Imagine waking up to passive income every month.
Imagine actually looking forward to your workouts.

name: I Bet You Can't
trigger: Dare provokes the urge to prove you can
template: I bet you can't [DO X] without [REACTION]
why_it_works: A challenge triggers the competitive urge to watch and beat it.
examples: I bet you can't watch this without pausing to write one down.
Bet you can't do this.
Try not to laugh.
I challenge you to make it to the end.

applied_examples.hook: I bet you can't hold this for 30 seconds.
I bet you can't guess which one is the drugstore dupe.

name: You Don't Need X, You Need This
trigger: Reframes the real lever behind a goal
template: You don't need [ASSUMED REQUIREMENT], you need [REAL FIX]
why_it_works: Removes a perceived barrier and replaces it with a simpler answer.
examples: You don't need more time, you need this trick.
You don't need the gym, try this 5-minute workout.
You don't need talent, you need this system.
You don't need more followers, you need this.

applied_examples.hook: You don't need supplements, you need to fix this.
You don't need more ads, you need this one offer.

name: This Free Tool Is A Game Changer
trigger: Free + transformative promises high-value utility
template: This free tool does what I used to pay $[X] for
why_it_works: Free combined with replacing a paid cost feels like found money.
examples: This free tool is a game changer!
This free tool does what I paid $200/month for.
You need this free tool for [task].
This AI saves me hours every week.

applied_examples.hook: This free tool writes a week of captions in 60 seconds.
This free tool handles all my booking, no more no-shows.

name: Come With Me To
trigger: Invites viewer along on an unfolding activity
template: Come with me to [DO ACTIVITY]
why_it_works: First-person invitation makes viewers feel like a companion.
examples: Come with me to set up my new studio.
Come with me to my first client meeting.
A day in my life, the real version.
What it's really like behind the scenes.

applied_examples.hook: Come with me to open my cafe at 5am.
Come with me to restock my entire salon.

name: This One Move Beats
trigger: Single action outperforms a harder alternative
template: This one [ACTION] does more than [HARDER THING]
why_it_works: Promises maximum result from minimal effort, an irresistible trade.
examples: This one move burns more than burpees.
This one habit made everything easier.
This one tweak beats hours of work.
The one thing I never skip.

applied_examples.hook: This one stretch does more than an hour of yoga.
This one habit beats any planner app.

name: Here's What Actually Works
trigger: Cuts through noise with the proven truth
template: Here's what actually works (and what doesn't) with [TOPIC]
why_it_works: Positions the creator as the filter between hype and reality.
examples: Here's what actually works (and what doesn't).
The truth about what actually works with [topic].
Forget the hype, here's what really matters.
Here's what finally worked for me.

applied_examples.hook: Here's what actually works for belly fat (not what you think).
Here's what actually grows an account in 2026.
